steps: Go to https://console.firebase.google.com/ and select your Firebase project (or create one)
Click the gear icon next to 'Project Overview' in the left sidebar and select 'Project settings'
Scroll down to the 'Your apps' section and click '</>' to add or select a web app
In the SDK setup and configuration panel, select 'Config'
Copy the value of the 'apiKey' field from the firebaseConfig object
This Web API key is passed as the ?key= query parameter on every Identity Toolkit request

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Firebase Web API Key
credential_fields.description: The Web API key from your Firebase project settings (Project settings > Your apps > Config > apiKey)

notes: The Firebase Web API key is project-scoped, not user-scoped — one key per project
This key is safe to use in client-side code for Authentication endpoints only
Requests go to identitytoolkit.googleapis.com with the key as a ?key= query param
Firebase has a generous free tier (Spark plan) with no billing required for Authentication
The key is visible in your web app's firebaseConfig and is not considered a secret for Auth endpoints

test_endpoint.description: A POST with no body returns a 400 INVALID_ID_TOKEN error, confirming the key is valid and the endpoint is reachable
